digest.component_or_area: test-coverage remediation epic (compaction, durability, CLI/MCP validation)
propositions: The compaction rollback branch in compact.py unconditionally removes the snapshot; it must retain the snapshot on incomplete rollback with a fault-injection test.
Whitespace-only ticket titles are wrongly accepted (the `if not title` check) and must be rejected across CLI, library, and MCP create/edit paths.
Compaction fold must be provably all-or-none under forward- and reverse-rename failure and recoverable after real process death.
Async-push, remote-append, and parent-cascade contracts need tests exercising actual process/clone-level behavior rather than in-process simulations.
Stale docs must be corrected: concurrency.md detached-HEAD test reference, README fcntl+mkdir lock framing, and mutation-testing.md score scoping.
The concurrency-storm xfail self-disable finding is invalid at the pinned commits and excluded from work.
